data.title: As a skill author, I can generate eval scaffolds from SKILL.md files
data.description:
## Description

**What**: A generate-skill-eval.sh script that reads a skill's SKILL.md and produces a starter evals/promptfooconfig.yaml with TODO-marked llm-rubric assertions.

**Why**: Agents modifying skills need a structurally valid starting point for eval configs. Copy-paste from examples leads to inconsistent structure and missing assertions.

**Scope**:
- IN: SKILL.md parsing (first H2 section or frontmatter description field, fence-aware), YAML generation with TODO-marked llm-rubric assertions, overwrite protection, atomic writes (temp+mv), YAML special character escaping, --help usage
- OUT: --force and --dry-run flags, eval execution, commit-time guard (S2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a skill author can run generate-skill-eval.sh <skill-name> and receive an evals/promptfooconfig.yaml in the skill's directory with correct provider/grader boilerplate and 1-2 TODO-marked llm-rubric assertions derived from the skill's SKILL.md description
  ← Satisfies: "generate-skill-eval.sh script accepts a skill name, reads its SKILL.md..."
- When this story is complete, the generator exits non-zero with a helpful message if the skill directory doesn't exist, the SKILL.md has no parseable description (neither frontmatter nor H2), or an evals/promptfooconfig.yaml already exists
  ← Satisfies: "exits non-zero with a helpful message..."
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Compatibility] Generated YAML must pass run-skill-evals.sh _validate_config() — TODO markers go in assertion values (type: llm-rubric, value: "TODO: ..."), not in structural positions (providers, tests keys must be valid)
- [Reliability] SKILL.md formats vary across ~30 skills — fence-aware H2 extraction must skip code blocks containing H2-like lines
- [Testing] YAML special character escaping is critical — colons, quotes, newlines from SKILL.md content must be properly escaped
